reviews.review_text: game used to run really badly, now it only runs moderately badly

It's definitely an enjoyable monster slaying action game with plenty to keep you busy for a good while. There's lots of gear to craft, items to collect, and creatures to capture. The game world is vibrant and many areas look very well done despite the somewhat washed out filtering going on throughout the game.

I liked having the mounts to speed up travel time and it's neat watching them wall run and jump between obstacles. The new combat features were also pretty sweet, as a dual blade character I really enjoyed the focus strikes. 

Somehow despite all of this I still find World to be the better game though I can't quite seem to put in to words why that is. I'd still say give this game a fair try though.

Played every game since MH3, with World being my favorite. I was ecstatic about Wilds being essentially World 2.0, but it has been largely a disappointment. Unfortunately, there's too many glaring issues to recommend the game.

My gripes have nothing to do with performance. I had around 60 fps on launch, and after toying around/light modding to get DLSS and frame gen to work with my GPU, I had a consistent 80-120 at all times, which I was fine with.

My disappointments were mainly with how much Capcom has dumbed down the game. They started adding QoL in World and Rise, and while some vets complained, most welcomed them. They saw the success those games brought and pumped that QoL up to 1000 for this game. Focus mode completely erases the methodical nature of MH combat, gathering or even grinding for monster parts (outside of ultra endgame gear) is a thing of the past, getting hit goes unpunished if you call your loyal steed to swoop in and save you, and my personal least favorite - the beautiful, sprawling maps the series has come to be known by have turned into glorified cutscenes.

I wasn't a fan of the mounts in Rise for a similar reason, but at least you were [i] looking at the screen [/i] while riding the palamute. Now when the monster goes to a new area, you [b] literally [/b] press tab and pick up your phone, alt tab, do WHATEVER YOU WANT, because the "hunting" part of monster hunter is a thing of the past. Why do monsters even change locations anymore? Or why not just fight everything in an arena and load in instantly? Some of these maps are gorgeous but I couldn't tell you a single thing about most of them.

Besides that, the endgame Artian weapon system (that they double downed on with TU4) is so mind-numbingly boring to interact with that I haven't bothered. I lost all interest when I saw multiple endgame build guides recommending savescumming to get the desired pieces. The game has a lot of great stuff- in particular I loved the ambitious approach to the MSQ. While it could've been much better, I've never been a huge fan of the "do this list of instanced quests" approach MH has done until now, and appreciated them trying something new. Furthermore, it has a great roster of beautiful monsters to fight, and the maps are on par with World for the most part. But too many poor decisions took away from the magic of MH and make it a chore to play.

One year later the game is better than ever! Launch was a complete disaster, but now? Everything is good, my performance issues are gone and there's tons of difficult content now to devour, including Final Fantasy XIV collaborations.

An incredible game that will only age like fine wine. Continuous support with title updates bringing new monsters, new power boosts, new ways to buildcraft, and new endgame metas. All while awaiting an expected DLC that with it will bring even more future support. This game is the best Monster Hunter has ever looked as well. I can't praise it enough. Just play. If you're new you will get humbled like this is a Souls game but the satisfaction of mastering a weapon, and a hunt you initially struggled with is unmatched. I'm talking some boses will take you days maybe weeks to beat solo like Savage Omega. The only other game to give me this feeling is Elden Ring except this is co-op. You can see other hunters everywhere in camps and join anyone's quests to help whenever you want. I love the community feeling you get being surrounded by other hunters. 

The only negative patch this game had were optimization issues that persisted for a while but slowly got better bit by bit with continuous updates. Largely right now things are much much better. The guys at Capcom acknowledged it was a strong pain point for many and have made strides. So I wouldn't let old reviews deter you too much. Give it a go and you won't be disappointed. The game will only improve more and more over time in terms of content and optimization as usual with all Monster Hunter games. I know my computer may be better than most but for reference I'm getting 170-180 FPS on average on ultra/high settings at 1440p with my specs which should be attached.
Still a great game and must play but definitely did not live up to Monster Hunter World.
Great game, still randomly crashes in 2026, over a year after the game came out.
I really enjoy this game more than Monster Hunter Worlds. Not having enough monsters to fight is its only problem.
Game constantly crashes. Despite what people say about optimizations making it run faster, I rarely ever had an issue with it crashing in the past and now it happens every 20 minutes.

-Update - Currently working within the same specs that I stated before. I have now unistalled and reinstalled the game with the exact same issues. I am beyond frustrated now and would really just like to say please for the love of everything holy FIX YOUR GAME!!! It should not be this hard for someone to enjoy a game that is part of a serious of beloved games that has a more than cult following. If this is the product that is thought to be acceptable then this will be the last monster hunter game I will be buying unless you can prove that my money is being spent on a stable and playable game. I truely love the game play and the story but I should not have to run this game on medium settings with the hardware I currently have and still not be able to continue the story due to instability and constant crashes. This is unacceptable and just down right sad. 

- Update - So far I have go through the normal checks. CPU power is 99%, core 0 and 1 are uncheck in affinity, Check file integrity, turned off frame gen, Have settings to medium, and things have gotten better but still not good. The game is telling me I should be in ultra settings so medium should be easy. I am still getting crashes randomly. may take 1 to 3 times the amount of time it would take originally but it is still crashing and now it can happen even when not in battle. This is still a fantastic game play and story wise but over all the user experience has been bad.

- I love this game overall for the story and the game play but this game crashes everytime i'm in a moment when i'm starting to win. I think total its crashed 15 times in less than 18 hours. I want to love this game so much but I just want to understand why. my system isn't low its pretty darn good so really there is no reason this is crashing as much as it is for medium settings other than the game wasn't built right.

Wilds is part of the reason I got myself a beefier PC. On max settings with 2k, the game still looks muddy, grainy and flat. 

Allow me to be unc for a minute. MH's vibrancy is a part of the series identity. These games used to be so colorful, so JUICY, albeit graphically unimpressive in the technical sense. The environments used to be inspiring and whimsical, the kind of thing you want to stop and take in. Here, they feel like duller, lesser imitations of what we saw in previous entries. Generic. It's odd to feel that way about a series that is otherwise overflowing with personality through its design.

Essentially:
Art direction > fidelity
Performance > fidelity

They bet on 'fidelity' and somehow made a game that not only runs like utter **** on the vast majority of systems, but is uglier than ever and therefore not even worth the performance trade-off. 

My first 20 hours of Wilds was experienced on my dated system, and it was perhaps the most torturous gaming experience I have ever had. I can finally give the game a real go with my new beast, but I will always be bitter about the lag fest Capcom made me go through just to get a taste of their latest hotdog water. We deserve better.

I feel less inclined to touch on gameplay, because my gripes are mainly with what I mentioned above. But, judging from the relatively small amount of time I committed to the game so far, I can confidently say I felt understimulated. I rarely ever got carted, and the monster was dead hilariously fast.

I’m not sure there’s much here for vets with hundreds of hours in the other games other than a time sink and a participation badge. MHGU is forever superior. And for an experience that is smooth and pretty, there’s MH World.

tldr: I have mixed feeling about the game.

-

(Thank you for reading and I'm sorry for my ESL)
Positive:
- The combat is fast, fun and very satisfying.
- The animation is great.
- The environment looks good.
- I really like the Savage Omega fight.
- The Seikret is cute

Neutral:
- Story is alright
- Endgame is alright. Can't expect to play one game forever.
- Features like monster tracking is simplified but it's fine.

Negative:
It feels like MHWorld went through plastic surgery and it's gone wrong.
In pursue of realism they abandoned any art-style they once had.
Any personality or any expression a Monster Hunter game has- is significantly reduced.
I know the older games has a long time to cook but surely they can pickup where they left off?
Did they lose all experience and knowledge gained from their last 1-3 games?

- The character customization is not great.
- The looks of weapons and armor is not great.
Why? if you know, you know.

- I think they forgot to optimize the game.
Could I have upgraded to a better CPU/GPU/Memory? Absolutely.
but there are so much better looking game that runs 100 times better than this game.
Too reliant on frame generation when there are no frames to generate from in the first place.
This is coming from a person who likes frame gen.

Ultimately, I still like the game but I feel like their focus is in the wrong place.
I kinda don't have hope for the DLC.
Maybe the next game will be better. 

(Maybe I'll be smart enough to not buy a game day 1 again lol)
Starting again on PC, had played through the base game on xbox. good enough to buy again and start all over.

Игра хороша, качество подтянули но всё ещё капризничает, фризы иной раз встречаются но фпс вырос заметно от того что было на релизе
В части гейм плея... 
Добавленный фокус мод добавил плавности кардинально в части игры по крайней мере мне (Long Sword main, GS), на GS тоже стало в разы проще и динамичнее...
Многие хают данную часть из-за позиционки внутри и как по мне очень зря (400ч World за спиной)
Файты стали плавнее иной раз ощущается как хорошие потанцульки
Что касается сложности, она на уровне, есть в этой игрульке монстрятина дающая прикурить по хлеще любого ранее виданного монстра в MH, Алатреон и Фаталис прикурят в сторонке
В остальном такой же гринд для крафта трусов из очередного монстра
Я после перехода из World перенастроив управление перехода не заметил

Good hunting hunters

The game itself is relatively fine, my gripe is with the Co-op.

My friend group saw it was a 4 player game and thought it would be a cool new one to try out (we've never played a game like this one before). Come to find out the process of playing together is so obnoxiously convoluted that it's not even worth playing.

You can't do actual missions together, you have to do the dialogue and npc following by yourself, then once there's a boss fight, it'll send an auto invite to anyone in your party to join (which isn't instantaneous btw, you'll have like 30-60 seconds of just you fighting by yourself at the very least; PLUS, it spawns your teammates at the base, not at the boss fight, so they have to run all the way to the fight, wherever it may be)

Oh, did you think you were gonna be able to just run around in the open environment together by just simply being in a party? You'd be absolutely right if it were any other game. This game requires you to be in a "link party" first, then be sure you've progressed at least an hour and a half through the game by yourselves, then you can make an "environment party", where you can just run around the open world together and fight basic monsters, no quest-related things, though.

I've never been so irritated trying to figure out how to simply play with my friends, and we would have all just returned the game but by the time we were able to finally get far enough to make it work, the 2 hour limit for returns was over.

If you're in my shoes wondering if you and your friends should get this game to play together, look elsewhere, it's genuinely not worth the headache to do barely anything with each other anyway.

So the core gameplay of the game is really solid, for the most part I love this game, but the state it's in is absolutely unacceptable for a full retail product. Crashes are constant, sometimes so severe that they require a full system restart. Assets failing to load is also common, controller configuration can break, and the game as a whole will not run reliably. For a game this far post-release, that is absolutely not acceptable.

Screw this game, although it is fun and has some interesting mechanics, the optimization of it is HORRIBLE and, worst of all the following:

I bought this to play WITH MY FRIENDS, and MOST OF THEM are having problems with connection, ONLY in this damn game. MH World works fine, but this one? NEVER WORKS, everytime we enter the same lobby and try to play a quest together, we never know if it's going to finish, cause 95% of the time, somebody gets "communication error" and gets kicked, even after trying ALL THE FIXES Capcom suggested.

So, because of this, screw this game...
Crashing in this game is like Schrodinger's Theory, the game is crashing, not crashing, and preparing to crash all at the same time (maybe it happens, maybe not)

After over a year I thought I'd take advantage of the sale and give it a shot, but sadly I still can't play this without getting a headache from constantly dropping below 40fps on low settings. Not even the cursed DLSS fixed that.

Granted I'm trying to play in 4k, but there's games out there that actually run in 4k native and above 100fps using max/high settings ...

Just constant crashes even still, tried optimizing it with nvidia lowering graphics, it didn’t even come close to using all my vram and was only using 80% gpu and 40% cpu, I do not recommend this game at all

It pains me to say this because if it wasn't for the horrible multiplayer system this game could be so much fun to play. Me and my wife were pretty pumped to play together only to realise that we put 50 bucks into a game that has no intention of an actual good multiplayer system in mind. From the amazing graphics and very fun weapon classes this game has so much potential but just destroys it with needing a degree to play with your buds.
The random GPU crash errors in the middle of a hunt and the connection problems when getting into quests are a big letdown that turn you off the game's immersion.

It took months for Capcom to properly address PC performance in Monster Hunter Wilds. Then it took even longer for them to add basic engine-level improvements that should have been in place at release. When those changes failed to meaningfully fix performance issues, they promised more performance updates.

Since then, they’ve released two performance patches, and both have fallen flat.

At least Capcom hasn’t completely abandoned the game the way they did with Dragon’s Dogma 2, but that’s not saying much. This is still a technical mess, and I can’t recommend it in its current state.

Stay away.

Can't even load into the game anymore. I just bought it on sale for $30.. should be worth $10 with the fact that it still runs poorly after all this time. Setting up a controller was a pain as well compared to Rise. I just don't understand how these developers could neglect the optimization of this game. I could request a refund, but from what I've seen I do want to play the game. I just wish it wasn't unpredictable and would run/load me into the game. Not even past the tutorial yet and I'm experiencing a bunch of issues. Over a whole year and this game is still a gamble on functionality. It's so disrespectful to the player/consumer.

EDIT: I finally got the game to load again, and I can say I like a lot of the changes and additions. The grapple hook to grab materials is handy and satisfying, and the game overall feels refined? Better? Whatever the word is. That said, I'm still leaving my review as is because that was my experience when first playing the game. Is this a completely terrible game, not at all. However, some things are still inexcusable. I look forward to playing more and getting ready for the DLC. Fingers crossed I don't run into any more issues.

Good game! Enjoyed it thoroughly! Monster hunter has a large place in my little animal autistic heart, and I for one think that this game is fun. PC specs aside, its a good next step in the MH series and I can't wait for the DLC/Expansion. Is it worth $70? No! But that's what games cost now! I remember when games were $40 and I thought that was a bit much then, too. But! Along with its flaws I've had the time of my life with my friends hunting and discovering new monsters. And to the haters, I leave you with this: relax. Its a video game.

The game is incredible, easily top 3 MH game i've played on my life. Other two being MH 4 and MH Generation ultimate. With that, said,  most of the game is easy but once you start getting the tempered versions, it gets tougher. The late game is brutal as well, try beating omega planets solo and it will be harder than dark souls. 

Graphics are incredible, i really like them despite some people saying they aren't that good.
Music - Incredible as always. MH team never disappoints.

Controls- They are hard, i don't game that often due to adult life but every time i play this game, i always forget the controls lmao, there's just so many stuff you can do with every weapon and inputs you gotta learn.  The learning curve is deep, so is gonna take a while if you don't game as much either. The old MH games from 3ds, wii and psp were way easier to learn compared to this one.

Performance wise - Runs great on my rig, used to barely hit 60 fps on max with many stutters a year ago but it finally got fixed after many updates lmao.

ive played every game since the ps2 and have to admit this one is one of the best!

the combat is smooth
upgrading weapons and armour isn't a massive grind and relitivly easy
the envitoments are beautiful
and its even goten my partner playing

i was debating even buying this game because of the absurd amount of negative reviews talking about the crashes and bad performance on the game and im not saying those people didnt have those experiences, but it seems capcom has fixed the major issues because my PC is straight cheeks and it still held up running MH Wilds. I didn’t even change any of the graphic settings from its default. The only thing I turned on was the reduce motion sickness option that was about it. Sure my PC was running the fan overtime and it was a bit hot but its been hotter while playing minecraft modpacks. i wont speak for everyone because i know there is always something that can go wrong but from my personal experience the game ran swimmingly. So all in all MH Wilds is a phenomenal game i absolutely love it. the graphics are superb and all the weapons are fleshed out catering to practically everyones needs with the 14 different weapon styles. i definitely recommend this game especially with the wild 58% discount going on right now


EDIT: After MH Wilds lets me have a wonderful time playing yesterday it gives me a huge slap in the face today. I played just fine for 4 hours straight yesterday but today i cant even open the game without it just closing on me not even 5-10 seconds after launching it. Phenomenal i can't even defend the game anymore

EDIT AGAIN: I got it working by i guess just adding some mods, i'm really not sure if it was the for sure cause of the solution but it hasn't suddenly closed the game since. i really just added the reframework and optimizer fps low cpu cleaner mod, its called something like that idk that mod has a long name. Its just weird that it worked so well yesterday but all of a sudden just closes my game on launch without even providing me a crash log so i wasn't even sure if it was crashing or just closing the game.

yeh  very good game watch my friend play  some part of it was like yeh im going to get this game so i wait for a big sell and boom 30$ dats plus tax it was 29.79 idk to lazy to check i was like dam now thats a good deal and bought it i do not regret it soooo if u see it on sell i say go for it great game so far its up there with world i wont lie

No performance issues at all, some say it still has.. Idk. 6700 XT and everything is max/ultra and whatever I can do (aside from obvious RTX type stuff) buttery performance and he game is really fun. Don't let the reviews deter you from a great game, especially on sale right now!

optimization still needs work, framegen should NOT be the solution, but it does make the game playable and the game itself is fun. id sadly call this a sidegrade to world rather than a full upgraded sequel mechanically, but the highs are pretty damn high and the QoLs are pretty damn QoL.

Beautiful graphic, nice costumes and battle system. However, the story is kind of boring and forgettable. If you are looking for a game that has a well-written story, don't bother to play this game. But if you're looking for a game that has great battle system, you might like this game.
game is so poorly optimized, it would be a good game if it didn't still have the issues with crashing after being out for so long. I think i'm done playing and waiting for it to work. such s shame.

This game is fun when you treat it like a standalone entry, but it's a whole different game from its predecessors.

As a spectacle fighter, it's fun. You have a lot of combos. The movement is clunkier than most spectacle fighters, but far from aggravating either. It nails the spectacle of the environment and the monster designs as the series always have. The performance is demanding, but you can get a stable 45 fps with mid-low end rig like mine at medium graphic settings. Stable 60 fps if you crank everything down. 

However, as I said, it's a spectacle fighter, not a Monster Hunter game. If you ever heard about the controversial change between Bioshock 1/2 and Bioshock Infinite, this is more or less the same case. Capcom removed any semblance of mandatory player agency this time around. The preparation and tracking side of the game are entirely omitted. 

You can resupply anywhere with fast travel, removing the need of limited inventory system (yet it is still there for some reason). Items are available in abundance, and consumables are pre-crafted for you. Everything is highly automated, so much so it surprises me that the combat remains manual. Even disengaging from monsters is now assisted extensively by the addition of all-purpose bail out vehicle.

There is no tracking anymore aside from some interactive cutscene. The contrast is like The Hunter hunting sim series and Duck Hunt arcade. The old tracking method of using paintballs gives you extra incentive to memorize monster habitats and their routine. Whether or not you enjoy this extra layer of gameplay depends on your stance towards immersive tediums. If you came from combat focused genre, MH will understandably feel like a clunkier version of your favorite game, with or without the tracking system.

However, Monster Hunter doesn't have to be another spectacle fighter or souls-like (as much as i love dark souls). Monster Hunter is always its own thing with its proudly distinct "prep-track-hunt" gameplay loop. I hope Capcom understand this.

[h3]GOOD GAME FOR WHAT IT'S WORTH[/h3]

For all the graphics issues that took a year to be addressed, this game is great for making me ride the [b]Great Maccao[/b] [strike](Yes, It's a decorated Seikret)[/strike]

[b]STILL! RIDING A GREAT MACCAO IS COOL![/b]

[spoiler]If Nata and forced Seikret moments aren't your cup of tea. Don't worry, it's not mine either[/spoiler]

Worth playing [u]if you don't compare it to previous games[/u]

Coming from Monster Hunter World, MH Wilds feels like a side-grade instead of a successor. Graphically this game feels like a downgrade compared to MH world. While the lighting might be better in this game everything feels muddy and blurry, so any higher details that might be present in this game is lost. Performance is also not that great considering what is on screen. 
Crashes are also very common. Going through a playthrough with a friend, it was a gamble on how many missions we could do in one sitting before one of us crashes. 
Playing with friends is also painful especially when trying to progress through story missions together. For some god awful reason you cant join someone else's quest until you've finished all the dialog/cutscenes, so you end up having to start the mission yourself and spend a couple minutes watching cutscenes just to watch them again while you're playing with someone else. This was also an issue with MH world but at least the game did not crash forcing you to do missions all over again.
You would think by now over a year after release they could figure out how to not have the game crash every 20-30 minutes, or fixing how god awful the experience is trying to play with with a friend. As of right now I can not recommend this to anyone unless they make the game stable enough to play, or if you get a deep discount off the price. If you're thinking of buying this I would suggest getting Monster Hunter World instead as it is a much better experience.

So for some context this is my very first ever monster hunter game, with that being said i absolutely love this game. its alot of fun and i never get tired of hunting giant monsters. and as someone with Arachnophobia the spiders in this game weirdly dont bother me at all
genuinely sucha fun game just only one issue with one singular monster. omega planetes feels like a massive step up in difficulty from zoh shia. especially with its damage and speed.

I started playing MH with MH Freedom, and I'd love, from the bottom of my heart, to give this game a positive review, but I can't.

This game is railroading me so hard that I lost all integrity and involvement in this game. I have no freedom. Every experience is preset so far. I can't explore, think, do, or act on my own! Movement though the world is Automated! *Find the monster* literally pinpointed on the map! xd

The story so far is boring and not involving me as a player. I skip cutscenes and dialogue because it doesn't matter what I see or do. Every experience is preset! As an MH veteran, I don't care about fancy cutscenes. They lost what MH is about: good gameplay and the freedom of what and when to do stuff.

So far, I was not allowed or encouraged to explore the open world. No fetch or collecting quests. Railroad all the way!!! I hate to say it, but I am literally writing this review while having to follow my annoying accomplice through the forest with my fake horse for hours. Why not just let us meet there?

This game fails to make players feel invested in its story, treating them as if they are too dumb to find things out on their own. Old MH games were not perfect, but I had to think—just a little bit—to get things done.

This has an even worse story than MH World. I hope the correct it in the DLC

i had to download mods bc this game was looking aweful. i had no performance issues so far!

As a long time Monster Hunter fan, it hurts to leave a bad review, but this game just does not feel like a good direction for the series.  I have hundreds of hours each in MH4, Generations, Rise, and World.  However, I found myself tired of Wilds after only about 40 hours. 

The game itself is beautiful, and I never experienced the performance issues or the crashing that many people have reported.  My complains are mostly around the game itself.  Capcom has been trying to simplify Monster Hunter for a while now, and Wilds is the most extreme example yet.  Gathering and crafting is easy and just don't feel rewarding anymore. Hunts are short, and the automatic traveling system kills the feel of actually being in the environment.  I find myself just not having fun or the desire to play the way I have with other games in the series.
I really enjoy the game. While the performance can be spotty, I run the game well on my toaster with a solid 60 - 70 fps.
Favorite monster hunter game of mine. Love the story though some of the later introduced ones get a bit....out there. The main quest it self is amazing though and I loved playing it!

The game is overall good, but it is unreasonably difficult to get anything you want out of the RNG. I'm talking 400+ hours and tens of thousands of rolls and nothing. I feel like the RNG needs some work, I also feel like "perfect" dodges need to be fixed so you can't be damaged while perfect dodging. These are the two gripes I have with it.

Monster Hunter Wilds has a issues beyond just its poor optimization connected to graphics that are nearly comparable to ps3 games. Monster Hunter Worlds visuals have more clarity. (Art direction helps a great amount in making a game look appealing to the eyes.) 

Many of the Arenas (where you fight certain monsters) are tight, the camera works against you, and the clarity is horrid (Rey Dau arena where everything is blue and hard to see).

Quality of life changes are nice, however what is the point of sharpening our blades at ALL if we can just do it on our Seikret. At that point it is no longer a game mechanic. In old monster hunter games it was important to decide when you would sharpen your blade during the fury of attacks directed towards you and your teammates, which added to the challenge of the hunt. 

Plus we as hunters have grown stronger yet the monsters have remained weak. They are relatively easy to stun lock due to the new wounds we can give them in about 11 hits or less. 

Also that damn walking exposé that is throughout the game. I don't wanna walk and talk at some slow pace, let me just hunt. If the info is relevant put it in a damn cutscene or stop holding my hand and place it in the damn quest description. But since this game likes to spoon feed you, just tell me through my guide during my fight.

Speaking of spoon feeding, did you know that loot that use to be connected to cutting tails or targeting certain spots is widely available as regular loot from just a regular hunt? meaning they have reduced the need for repeated farming. All things considered a good change cause there is no fun in beating a damn baby seal.

Monster Hunter Wilds also as extensive issues with something that the studio for some reason wanted to focus on, the damn story. Monster Hunter Rise, if I am not mistaken is the first time we have had official named characters introduced into the franchise and in this game they continued on with this focus and even expanded on it. The characters in this game are insufferable to interact with and to look at. Nata is a horrid character that I wish I could let die when he wanted to run at that damn monster. I know that everyone loves Gemma, the butterface blacksmith, plus our damn horrid looking guide that I have the displeasure of having to deal with. You gooners need better taste. The oilwell basin had chicks with biceps larger than my skull. The characters that you deal with beyond the butterfaces are androgynous as all hell and make you want to avert your damn eyes.

The most horrid part of the story can be summarized like this: Nata asking why they deserved to be attacked when his damn village were literally playing God and creating artificial monsters. Hell they should have been wiped out.

I understand the story doesn't matter "it's a Monster Hunter game" THEN WHY FOCUS ON IT AT ALL.

Now on a side note, if you thought this game in any way or form was anti-agenda you will find that you are mistaken. Just look at the character designs and who takes the center stage constantly. Plus the use of body type A/B(1/2), rather than Male and Female.

Wilds is a great game that makes a bad first impression.

It has really interesting lore that the plot railroads and rushes you through.
It has extremely deep combat and buildcraft mechanics that are (initially) overshadowed by the wound system.
It has a rich ecosystem that you are never required to engage with.

Once the game opens up in High Rank, the lore gets to shine more.
Once the later-game monsters become more resistant to wounds, the rest of the combat system fits into place.
Once you fiddle with the settings and disable Seikret auto-movement, the world is actually a nice place to wander and gather in.

The game is constantly in tension with what it wants to be, but most of the friction that comes from attempting to appeal to newer players is temporary or opt-out, and it rewards you sticking with it. I firmly believe late-game Wilds is better than World, and the Wilds expansion has the potential to be the best Monster Hunter has ever been.

If you can run it, of course. But my PC used to regularly run out of polygons running this game, and now runs it smoothly as of the last title update.

[i]With the last Achievement secured, I finally feel qualified enough to give my thoughts on MHWilds.[/i]

This is my first Monster Hunter Game and having experienced it fully,
[b]I heartly recommend this title to other curious newcomers[/b] <3

Whatever motivation compels you to pick the game/series up, you'll find something here:
[list]
[*] The unique Monsters
[*] The thrill of a challenging hunt
[*] FARMING for better Weapons & Equipment
[*] or Cosmetics (Fashion-Hunter)
[*] Running around Collecting/Gathering/Fishing, whilst enjoying the Environments*
[*] Multiplayer with fellow Hunters & Friends~
[/list]
The story isn't anything groundbreaking, it's simple & sweet, and enjoy how the *ecosystem ties in with the plot. The characters are fairly shallow but their personality & interactions makes up for it imo. There's also one case of character development that's quite sweet.

Can't speak about the performance issues cause it ran alright from the getgo and I'm not tech savvy when it comes to specs. But ignoring the optimization issues the PC Version clearly has, it's quite the enjoyable game.

Lastly, I don't know what it is about this title in particular but I always get so immersed that I lose track of time. I genuinely love this game for that.

12 hours of gameplay at the time of review. 11 of those hours have been messing with settings, getting Vortex for the FPS boost mod, and looking things up as well as installing new drivers and updates and doing literally anything possible to get the FPS past 50. 

It's not even the graphics, it's the insanely laggy and horrible optimization of the game that drags it down. It's such a hardcore CPU bottleneck because of all the unnecessary ♥♥♥♥ happening in the background that you can't see lol. 

Edit: If you change your graphics from 1920 to 1280 and the FPS doesn't increase, it's a dead giveaway for being a CPU bottleneck as I mentioned above. Meaning that the graphics ARE NOT the things responsible for your frames. Look through settings for anything with a CPU rating on it. I lowered render distance, reduced animations, lowered endemic life count, reduced mesh detail (kept texture quality at high) and reduced foliage quality and swaying to the minimum. All this and I was still capped at 55fps. NVIDIA AI frame generation is a godsend.

Least fun MH game I've played. A lot of weapons don't benefit from the full move set, locking you to 1, maybe 2 different paths. The upgrade system is still supper linear, though this isn't new. The end game is not super engaging and the Gogma weapons as a system are kind of just rng BS. I enjoyed Rise quite (though, anomaly rolls are BS) a bit more, and even World was more engaging, at least.

The graphics aren't good enough to justify the watering down of the mechanics, and there are still visual bugs and performance drops that occur to this day, even on lowest settings

Game's been out a year and three months and I still get graphical bugs and hard crashes.... Fantastic gameplay. I just wish the thing ran properly. Have to restart the game about once an hour when playing with friends.

Played on release, it barely ran but at least it ran. Stopped at 91hrs playtime. Came back to the game last week because I just really wanted to play some Monster Hunter. Game crashed like 2hrs in. Did some research, updated my BIOS and drivers, tweaked virtual memory settings. Seemed to help at first but then it went back to crashing after 2-3 hunts. Do not buy this or it's DLC.

--EDIT--

Got some more help with settings and optimization, and now the game runs pretty well but will still crash occasionally. Still do not recommend a purchase, especially if you are using AMD 6000 series cards as apparently they have the worst time running this game.

Do not buy this game. Capcom has been playing fast and loose with performance for a while, but the game will actually be unplayable after 5 hours on any AMD graphics cards. Capcom knows about this, and their solution is to recommend year old AMD drivers that do not solve the problem. I played 90 hours on a 2070, then upgraded to AMD, only to require my graphics to be lower than my 2070 could run with frequent crashes. Look into any other RE Engine games and you will see that almost all of them cause "fatal d3d error 24 dxgi_error_device_removed" with AMD cards. People will tell you to undervolt your card, reduce frequency, turn off upscaling techniques, clear your shader caches, etc. The only solution that buys you time is deleting your shader cache files, for me specifically I have to delete DXCCache every time I launch the game to play a single hunt. Capcom does not care about you or your crashes. They got you already: they gave you a look at their game, dropped a title update, bricked it for many people and locked the door. These crashes conveniently can be attributed to hardware malfunction or software corruption, and Capcom wields that excuse like an axe. In fact, you might find most people on the internet describe this as a client-side problem; it isn't. This game worked before they "fixed performance."

This game performs laughably badly on PC.  It stutters constantly at the lowest settings while MH world, Sunbreak and other games run absolutely fine.  The game is very good and I have played it on PS5, but PC I would say stay away until Capcom actually optimizes it to run at a reasonable performance.

How do you make this much low ressed blurry textures perform this bad like even using dlss and frame gen the game isnt stable and the bigger problem is game looks bad in every option with or without dlss 2k or 4k with high res textures everytime it looks ♥♥♥♥ how why you even bother to release this at this point

My least favorite Monster Hunter game to date. Maybe an expansion would change that; as I felt Rise had a bland base game but a stellar Expansion with Sunbreak. I never experienced major performance issues, but the gameplay just lacked that old spirit that made the previous games great.

Still waiting for the performance improvement to this day.
I love this game, NGL but it took a toll on my PC

My list of issues with this game:

1. At the time of release, content was lacking. It took them a lot of time to add enough to the game so that it was actually acceptable for a MH game. Examples: lack of replayable Zoh Shia mission, lack of a Hunter Hub, etc. The experience across the lifecycle of the game was kinda poor overall. Endgame is somewhat enjoyable, though.

2. Poor performance even after the last updates (some areas of the map still at 50-ish FPS on 1440p, specific monster attacks make my GPU choke for long enough to be disrupting to gameplay, etc.). We waited A LOT until things improved, and even then, the improvements were modest. This new engine (RE Engine) is cursed.

3. Atrocious multiplayer model that gives cryptic errors every mission or another, since release, and to this day. They can appear anywhere: during initial game loading, during login, before and during missions, etc. Sometimes I can't even login or post missions in a lobby, whereas I can create/join World and Rise multiplayer with no issues whatsoever. I suspect Crossplay is the culprit, but I have no idea anymore. Also, having only 4 players like in Rise is not enough, but I don't know if having up to 100 players in a single lobby is the best idea. Note that the errors are generally server-side ones, as far as I can tell from my research. My network is configured as Capcom recommends. Disabling Crossplay or chainging regions on Steam improve stability, but eventually, they happen again.

4. The amount of RNG in this game is insane too, and I refuse to save-scum or use mods. I'd prefer if they took an approach of farming for specific items to create equipment (even if only to increase chances), instead of depending too much on lootbox mechanics. This is not a gacha game.

5. Expanding on the previous point: I dislike and disengage from the Artian endgame whenever possible, for the reasons already stated.

6. Overtuned difficulty in the endgame. The impression I have is that adjustments were a knee-jerk reaction to criticism of the game being "too easy." It seems difficulty is just more moster HP + more damage + one-hit KOs, which is just a band-aid over an issue that's deeper than just minor tweaks. It seems something in the gameplay makes it hard to balance hunter's abilities and monster's challenge. Too many mechanics, perhaps?

7. Can we consider Gogmazios a siege fight? If so, that's not what I was expecting. I wish we have old Siege fights like before, with a shared goal across missions. Also, I dislike Gogmazios' fight: too much spectacle, too many huge attacks, area-of-denial everywhere, and too much craziness going on. Multiplayer is unstable for me, so sometimes I can't even complete missions before some random error pops up. If you bring Support Hunters, they completely steal the moment of the fight, and when you least expect, Proof of a Hero is playing and you're not even in the arena. Luckily, I'm not farming Artian, so I can just ignore Gogmazios altogether.

8. Excessive skills and mechanics. I have the impression that the base game has too much "stuff" going on, which maybe shows a lack of focus? Most of the systems and skills should have been introduced on G-rank instead.

9. Minor pet-peeve: The lack of a truly viable Health Augment mechanic in the endgame is also something that disappoints me. PLEASE, Capcom, bring it back on the expansion. I don't care if it's a bit less effective than in World, just bring it back.

I hope they improve things for G-rank, in which case I'll revise this review, but so far, I'm not holding my breath. I'll wait some time, and also for reviews, before buying the expansion. I must confess that, even though I'm disappointed with Wilds, I'm still hopeful the team can turn things around, but I can't give anything but a negative review in good/honest conscience.

Monster Hunter Wilds has been an amazing experience for me so far. The world feels alive, the monsters are incredible, and every hunt feels exciting. Combat is as satisfying as ever, and the new mechanics make battles feel even more dynamic.

One thing I really appreciate is how much the game has improved since the beta. Performance and stability are noticeably better, making the overall experience much smoother than what I expected.

That said, my GTX 1650 laptop definitely struggles at times. I have to play on lower settings, and there are occasional frame drops, especially in busy areas and some cutscenes. The overexposure issue during certain cutscenes can also be distracting.

Even with those limitations, I'm having a great time. The gameplay loop is incredibly addictive, and every new monster feels worth hunting.

Rating: 7.5/10 (GTX 1650 Laptop)
A fantastic Monster Hunter game that shows huge improvements from the beta, even if older hardware like mine can't fully keep up with its ambitions. 🐉⚔️

performance is much better than on initial launch, at least for me; i know there are some lingering errors for AMD users, which is a shame. the voice acting is broadly pretty solid, definitely a step up from world, and the fights to me--and to be fair i'm a new gen player, so older fans may disagree--are engaging through and through. i mourn the tracking system from world and i wish the world map were not designed with seikret autopilot in mind, but the game is enjoyable nonetheless.

I've played both Monster Hunter Wilds and Dauntless, and honestly I had more fun with Dauntless.

Don't get me wrong, MH Wilds is a great game, but I realized I prefer a simpler gameplay loop. In Dauntless, you spawn in the hub, upgrade your gear, queue up with other players, hunt a monster, and repeat. It's straightforward and easy to jump into.

With MH Wilds, the open world, all the systems, and the countless little mechanics ended up feeling more exhausting than enjoyable for me. I can definitely see why people love it, but it just wasn't my thing.

That's why I'm really hoping Horizon Hunters Gathering ends up being more like Dauntless easy to pick up, focused on the hunting experience, and with less complexity getting in the way.

good game if you can get past running a lower framerate than what you might be used to.  It doesnt run that poorly, actually.  I normally run 240-180, but this game I generally run 90-60 (which is still really good in my book).  It's never dropped below 60 for me.  It's hard on your CPU but they have settings to minimize that.

As for gameplay, that is ALSO really good.  I think the monsters could do with more health early on, but that's about it.

Will return after i play more, but so far the combat's the best in the entire series despite all hunts ending in 4-5 minutes; probably a low rank thing since the Hunters are so OP.

Focus mode is completely busted, it completely erases the need for proper positioning and spacing in weapons like Greatsword and Longsword, and despite some people claiming its "optional" all weapons have attacks tied to them, so if you wanna use your weapon's full capacity thats needed; criticizing focus mode despite using it doesnt make criticism any less valid; you cant properly criticize a book without reading it, a game without playing it etc.

Will return once i do more of it cause the combat's very fun and the visuals are pleasing, hopefully i'll get past the boring phase soon.
I did enjoy this more than worlds, ngl, they nailed the introduction for new players to the genre. Now it's not perfect but hopefully with ascendance rework it will get better! still worth it

Quickly writing this review after a bit of playing and testing on Steam Deck, just so you can know what its like. My steam deck is one of the old models, so not an OLED one.

Framerate hits between 35-40fps in combat, and about 45fps in cutscenes. Graphics look a little shabby on the ends but its not absolutely game ending for me personally, since ive been playing since the 3DS era and have never cared too much for graphics. You get a warning when opening the game about controls, but when youre in the game, the controls are just fine. You can see the steam deck buttons on the overlay, and everything pretty much works the same way as other monster hunter games. If youre super nit picky about graphics and framerate, you may want to wait for another future update (hopefully) to optimize things for the deck. But me personally im just glad the game runs fine, and im sure a few others share the same sentiment. (Ive heard people on OLED say they can hit up to 60fps if that helps too)

Features one of the better stories i.m.o. for a Monster Hunter title. Something something ecosystem isn't in balance, go fix.

Combat is more fluid as compared to World, but that also makes it more forgiving, would have loved more challenge in the early game.  Later, you'll have the option to challenge Arch-Tempered versions of previous monster, which did kick my butt. Moreover, I personally wasn't a big fan of some monster species--without spoiling much, I would've loved more 'four legged kind'.

Base game MH:Wilds has two good maps, being the steppes and the forest--however, the two other maps are primarly underground and feel bland and not lived in. MH:World had more variety, each having a unique local and living fauna.

The visuals look good, but the performance--even after all the performance patches--is not good. I would've been contempt with stable 60fps, but even then it dips at some intense scenes. I understand (?) the engine wasn't meant for the big open world areas, but I expected more from a mainline series from CAPCOM. Performance issue DID tire me, to the point I didn't feel connected with the game.

As it stands now, I'll give it a 6,7/10--in particular it's (1) the performance and (2) the maps that don't do it well enough for me.

Been passionately playing monster hunter since MH3U (hunting horn main).
First, let me explain that this game had a ROUGH launch for pc! Not only the optimization, it was the weapon tuning, input delays, difficulty, lack of monsters within the open world and less content compared to every other monster hunter titles. The game felt barren after you were shotgun through low rank/storyline into high rank. You were forced into the storyline until it was finished. Before the title updates, the game didn't have much to do afterwards and left a deep scar into Wilds from previous monster hunter communities.

   After playing from launch to title update 4, I believe Capcom has done what I felt to be a validated come back from these title updates. They fixed their optimization issues, added more event quests, tuned up weapons, added more monsters and scaled up late high-rank difficulties (You will face challenges).
The dev team did their best to listen to the Wilds community from the rough launch and they did deliver. Their efforts were genuine and I fully hope the DLC is to be something that the community is all hoping for.

I recommend catching this on sale and maybe bringing a friend or 2 along the way.
